name: Warming Frost
description: A high ridge region where frost clings to the ground year-round despite the temperate climate below. The corruption here manifests as warmth — a creeping, sedative heat that works against the cold, and against clarity.
ambient_lines: The frost doesn't melt underfoot. Your boot prints are warm for a moment, then the ice seals over them.
A warm current moves through the cold air — not wind. Something directional.
Ice crystals on the pine needles hold impossible colors at the right angle. You look away.
The corruption here smells sweet. That's new. That's worse.
Your breath fogs, but the frost-touched air that enters your lungs feels almost pleasant.
A frozen stream. The water beneath the ice is moving. The fish in it glow faintly.
Something has been nesting in the frost. The depression is body-shaped and wrong.
The cold should bother you more than it does. You notice you've stopped shivering.
A cave opening in the ridge face. Warm air comes from inside. You do not enter.
The corruption's warmth concentrates near the ridge spine. You can feel exactly where it's strongest.
Ice formations shaped like hands, faces — natural, probably. You tell yourself natural.
The sky here is always pale. Whatever filters the light, it does it completely.
Your companions walk slightly closer together on this stretch without discussing it.
A dead lupine in the frost, perfectly preserved. It wears an expression of completion.
The crystals on the ground ring like glass when the wind moves them. Music, almost.
You find yourself slowing down without meaning to. The cold that should push you forward doesn't.
A patch of unfrozen ground, perfectly circular, three meters across. You walk around it.
Something has carved a shallow channel in the frost in a straight line for as far as you can see.
The warmth in the air pulses. Very slowly. Like breathing.
Standing still for more than a moment, you feel the corruption pressure at the edges of your thoughts.

time_variants.dawn: At dawn, the frost catches light in ways that make distance unreliable. Everything looks closer than it is.
time_variants.morning: The morning brings a brief window of clarity — air sharp, corruption pressure lower. It lasts an hour.
time_variants.midday: Midday is the worst. The warmth-corruption peaks when the sun is highest, as if it's drawing from it.
time_variants.evening: Evening on the Warming Frost carries a particular loneliness — the cold returns, but the wrongness in it doesn't leave.
time_variants.night: At night the frost glows. Not brightly. Just enough that you can see it doing it.

points_of_interest.name: The Ridge Spine
points_of_interest.desc: The highest point of the Warming Frost. Passive corruption peaks here. Standing on it, you can see three regions. Kasyrra's cult has placed markers at each cardinal point.

points_of_interest.name: The Warm Cave
points_of_interest.desc: A cave system running deep into the ridge. The warmth inside is body-temperature exact. It is very comfortable. No one who has gone inside has reported what they found.

points_of_interest.name: Frost Shrine
points_of_interest.desc: A small shrine of black ice and silver offerings. The offerings are recent. Someone is actively worshipping something here.

arrival_scene: The temperature drops as you climb toward the Warming Frost — and then, inexplicably, stops dropping. The cold remains, precise and structural, but something underneath it is warm in the way that pressure is warm, the way a hand pressed flat against skin is warm. You realize you have been here for two minutes and you already don't entirely want to leave.

connections.name: Wolf-Road Edge
connections.desc: South and down the ridge. The cold thins fast once you begin descending.

connections.name: Imp Warren Entrance
connections.desc: Northwest, where the frost meets the rocky terrain. The imps are worse in cold weather.
